title: Juno: New Origins
anchor_summary: Physics-heavy aerospace sandbox focused on designing customizable vehicles, testing them across air/land/space, and progressing through contracts, milestones, and tech unlocks.

similar_games.why_similar: Build-and-fly space program sandbox with realistic orbital mechanics, staging, missions, and engineering-driven progression.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify Juno: New Origins as a 3D aerospace sandbox centered on designing, building, testing, and iterating vehicles with realistic physics and orbital mechanics. Core play is construction and simulation: customizable parts, engine tuning, fuel and flight systems, programming automation, planet creation, and persistent sandbox experimentation. Career mode adds contracts, milestones, money, and a tech tree, but these sit on top of the primary engineering sandbox loop rather than changing the genre. There is no combat; challenge comes from simulation realism, vehicle control, and optimization. This strongly matches engineering_sandbox_sim.
